name: Interior Archetype - Inn
required_conditions: The interior is larger than a single small room and combines at least two functionally distinct zones under one roof (a common/gathering area and a lodging area).
The interior has exactly one primary exterior entrance/exit, even though it may contain multiple interior sub-zones.
The interior's function is lodging-for-hire or a comparable multi-purpose gathering-plus-private-space building.
The interior is reached from a town or village exterior map.

provenance.notes: Confidence inherited directly from the source pattern: corroborated by exactly one official sample map (Map020.json). Do not report this archetype as High confidence in a citing implementation contract. If the interior is genuinely single-room, use ARCH-INTERIOR-SHOP or ARCH-INTERIOR-HOUSE instead -- this archetype's zoning rules do not apply to an undivided room. Gap: PAT-INTERIOR-INN does not evidence one strong single-object dominant landmark (unlike the fireplace this project later hand-built into Map026, which is a project-specific addition, not part of the official-sample-derived pattern) -- the landmark_slots entry above is marked non-required/non-dominant rather than asserting one that current evidence does not support, per WO-0059's own instruction to record unsupported desired variation as an explicit candidate, not an accepted fact.
